title: [Docs] FAQ: OneDrive restore key is missing a character (DisableFileSyncNGS → DisableFileSyncNGSC)
author: ace4x0
body:
<!-- 
PLEASE READ THIS CAREFULLY:

1. You *MUST* read and complete the steps from the checklist below, by placing
   an x into each [ ] (so that it shows '[x]', NOT '[ x]' or '[x ]'), BEFORE
   clicking on 'Submit new issue'.

2. Failure to perform these steps, WHICH ARE ONLY THERE TO HELP *YOU*, will
   usually result in your issue being dismissed without notice.

3. If you are reporting an issue when trying to run Rufus, or when trying to
   boot a media created by Rufus, you *MUST* provide a log, period. Please do
   not assume that the developer(s) will be able to "guess" the specifics of
   your environment, what image you used, what type of media you used it with
   or the many many other critical parameters that the log provides data for.
   To investigate an issue, a log from Rufus is ALWAYS required.

4. If you still *choose* not to provide a log when reporting a problem, you
   agree that your issue will be closed without any further investigation.

YOU HAVE BEEN WARNED.

Checklist
---------
- [x] I have been made aware that if my problem can be summarized as _"I've created or tried to create a media using Rufus, and ..."_, and I am not including a log, **this issue will be summarily closed**.
- [x] I looked at https://github.com/pbatard/rufus/wiki/FAQ to see if my question has already been answered.
- [x] I performed a search in the issue tracker for similar issues using keywords relevant to my problem, such as the error message I got from the log.
- [x] I clicked the 'Log' button (🗒️) or pressed <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>-<kbd>L</kbd> in Rufus, or used [DebugView](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sysinternals/downloads/debugview), and copy/pasted the log into the section that says `<FULL LOG>` below.
- [x] The log I am copying is the FULL log, starting with the line `Rufus version: x.y.z` - I have NOT removed any part of it.

Additionally (if applicable):
- [ ] I ran a bad blocks check, by clicking _Show advanced format options_ then _Check device for bad blocks_, and confirmed that my USB is not defective.
- [ ] I also tried one or more of the following:
  - [ ] Using a different USB drive.
  - [ ] Plugging the USB into a different port.
  - [ ] Running Rufus on a different computer.
- [ ] If using an image, I clicked on the `(✓)` button to compute the MD5, SHA1 and SHA256 checksums, which are therefore present in the log I copied. I confirmed, by performing an internet search, that these values match the ones from the official image.

Issue description
-----------------
**Rufus:** 4.15.2396
**Source ISO:** Win11_25H2_v2_English_x64 (build 26100.8037)

Two small things from reading the generated `sources\$OEM$\$$\Panther\unattend.xml`
against the FAQ. First is a definite typo; second is a genuine question, not a complaint.

---

## 1. Typo - "How can I restore OneDrive after using the QoL option?"

The FAQ currently says:

> ...you need to go to set the `HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\OneDrive\DisableFileSyncNGS`
> back to `0`.

The value name is missing its trailing **C**. It should be **`DisableFileSyncNGSC`**.

This is confirmed two ways:

- The paragraph immediately above it in the same FAQ names the key correctly
  ("Rufus sets the `DisableFileSyncNGSC` registry key").
- The generated `unattend.xml` writes:
  ```
  reg add "HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\OneDrive" /v DisableFileSyncNGSC /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
  ```

Someone following the restore instructions literally might be confused.

---

## 2. Question - taskbar search box

The QoL option also writes:

```
reg add "HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Search" /v SearchboxTaskbarMode          /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
reg add "HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Search" /v SearchboxTaskbarModeCache     /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

which collapses the taskbar search box to a search icon.

I couldn't map this cleanly onto any of the items in the FAQ list. It isn't an ad, a
news feed, a Start Menu pin, or a forced app. It may well be intended as part of the
general taskbar/Start cleanup, in which case this is nothing.

**Is it considered covered by an existing entry, or would it be worth listing?** Entirely
your call; flagging only in case it was unintentional.

---

Method, for what it's worth: generated `unattend.xml` twice from the same ISO with
identical settings, toggling only the QoL checkbox, and diffed. Everything else in the
FAQ list matched what QoL actually applies, including the Start Menu shortcuts
(`VisiblePlaces`) and Fast Startup (`HiberbootEnabled`). The documentation is accurate;
these are the only two items I had anything to say about.

title: Parts of custom ISO appears to be corrupted on the drive
author: Compaszer
body:
<!-- 
PLEASE READ THIS CAREFULLY:

1. You *MUST* read and complete the steps from the checklist below, by placing
   an x into each [ ] (so that it shows '[x]', NOT '[ x]' or '[x ]'), BEFORE
   clicking on 'Submit new issue'.

2. Failure to perform these steps, WHICH ARE ONLY THERE TO HELP *YOU*, will
   usually result in your issue being dismissed without notice.

3. If you are reporting an issue when trying to run Rufus, or when trying to
   boot a media created by Rufus, you *MUST* provide a log, period. Please do
   not assume that the developer(s) will be able to "guess" the specifics of
   your environment, what image you used, what type of media you used it with
   or the many many other critical parameters that the log provides data for.
   To investigate an issue, a log from Rufus is ALWAYS required.

4. If you still *choose* not to provide a log when reporting a problem, you
   agree that your issue will be closed without any further investigation.

YOU HAVE BEEN WARNED.
-->

Checklist
---------
- [x] I have been made aware that if my problem can be summarized as _"I've created or tried to create a media using Rufus, and ..."_, and I am not including a log, **this issue will be summarily closed**.
- [x] I looked at https://github.com/pbatard/rufus/wiki/FAQ to see if my question has already been answered.
- [x] I performed a search in the issue tracker for similar issues using keywords relevant to my problem, such as the error message I got from the log.
- [x] I clicked the 'Log' button (🗒️) or pressed <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>-<kbd>L</kbd> in Rufus, or used [DebugView](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sysinternals/downloads/debugview), and copy/pasted the log into the section that says `<FULL LOG>` below.
- [x] The log I am copying is the FULL log, starting with the line `Rufus version: x.y.z` - I have NOT removed any part of it.

Additionally (if applicable):
- [x] I ran a bad blocks check, by clicking _Show advanced format options_ then _Check device for bad blocks_, and confirmed that my USB is not defective.
- [x] I also tried one or more of the following:
  - [x] Using a different USB drive.
  - [x] Plugging the USB into a different port.
  - [ ] Running Rufus on a different computer.
- [ ] If using an image, I clicked on the `(✓)` button to compute the MD5, SHA1 and SHA256 checksums, which are therefore present in the log I copied. I confirmed, by performing an internet search, that these values match the ones from the official image.

Issue description
-----------------
I have been using rufus for writing the ISO of my own operating system to a USB drive for over a year now. But now I get some problems. I've put some more data in my initramdisk (around 30MiB) and suddenly the initramdisk is corrupted on the USB drive. When deleting some data from the initramdisk, it is uncorrupted again. The corrupted bytes are the same on every write attempt, even if I update the kernel in my ISO. I've written the same ISO with balenaEtcher to the same USB drive and there it is uncorrupted, so I assume my ISO to be correct.
The attached log shows several write attempts with two different USB drives and one write attempt with "check for defect blocks" activated.
My OS uses grub2 with multiboot2 if that is relevant.

You can find my iso at: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1SVZW5mBjhMakO8Nf6BKQYhZjgz-F9-D_/view?usp=sharing

Log

title: Parts of custom ISO appears to be corrupted on the drive
author: Compaszer
body:
<!-- 
PLEASE READ THIS CAREFULLY:

1. You *MUST* read and complete the steps from the checklist below, by placing
   an x into each [ ] (so that it shows '[x]', NOT '[ x]' or '[x ]'), BEFORE
   clicking on 'Submit new issue'.

2. Failure to perform these steps, WHICH ARE ONLY THERE TO HELP *YOU*, will
   usually result in your issue being dismissed without notice.

3. If you are reporting an issue when trying to run Rufus, or when trying to
   boot a media created by Rufus, you *MUST* provide a log, period. Please do
   not assume that the developer(s) will be able to "guess" the specifics of
   your environment, what image you used, what type of media you used it with
   or the many many other critical parameters that the log provides data for.
   To investigate an issue, a log from Rufus is ALWAYS required.

4. If you still *choose* not to provide a log when reporting a problem, you
   agree that your issue will be closed without any further investigation.

YOU HAVE BEEN WARNED.
-->

Checklist
---------
- [x] I have been made aware that if my problem can be summarized as _"I've created or tried to create a media using Rufus, and ..."_, and I am not including a log, **this issue will be summarily closed**.
- [x] I looked at https://github.com/pbatard/rufus/wiki/FAQ to see if my question has already been answered.
- [x] I performed a search in the issue tracker for similar issues using keywords relevant to my problem, such as the error message I got from the log.
- [x] I clicked the 'Log' button (🗒️) or pressed <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>-<kbd>L</kbd> in Rufus, or used [DebugView](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sysinternals/downloads/debugview), and copy/pasted the log into the section that says `<FULL LOG>` below.
- [x] The log I am copying is the FULL log, starting with the line `Rufus version: x.y.z` - I have NOT removed any part of it.

Additionally (if applicable):
- [x] I ran a bad blocks check, by clicking _Show advanced format options_ then _Check device for bad blocks_, and confirmed that my USB is not defective.
- [x] I also tried one or more of the following:
  - [x] Using a different USB drive.
  - [x] Plugging the USB into a different port.
  - [ ] Running Rufus on a different computer.
- [ ] If using an image, I clicked on the `(✓)` button to compute the MD5, SHA1 and SHA256 checksums, which are therefore present in the log I copied. I confirmed, by performing an internet search, that these values match the ones from the official image.

Issue description
-----------------
I have been using rufus for writing the ISO of my own operating system to a USB drive for over a year now. But now I get some problems. I've put some more data in my initramdisk (around 30MiB) and suddenly the initramdisk is corrupted on the USB drive. When deleting some data from the initramdisk, it is uncorrupted again. The corrupted bytes are the same on every write attempt, even if I update the kernel in my ISO. I've written the same ISO with balenaEtcher to the same USB drive and there it is uncorrupted, so I assume my ISO to be correct.
The attached log shows several write attempts with two different USB drives and one write attempt with "check for defect blocks" activated.
My OS uses grub2 with multiboot2 if that is relevant.

You can find my iso at: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1SVZW5mBjhMakO8Nf6BKQYhZjgz-F9-D_/view?usp=sharing

Issue description
-----------------
There are new "guardrails" in place for doing a silent Windows install (which I only figured out by looking at #2958), however they can be circumvented, kinda. When I enable all three required options (local account, regional options and data collection), I can enable the silent install and choose for example "Windows 11 Pro Education". When I then disable one of the required options (in my case local account, since I don't want that anyway), Windows is still silently installed, i. e. the "silent install"-option gets hidden but not disabled.

Although, being able to silently install without creating a local account would be great for me, but I understand if you want to remove that option.

Issue description
-----------------
Windows by default assumes the BIOS/UEFI time is local time. Most Linux distros instead prefer to work with the idea that the BIOS/UEFI time is UTC and apply the offset based on user selection.

This means on my system which is Linux bare metal and a Windows ToGo for some games I get time mismatches between booting both systems.

In this case I need to apply the "RealTimeIsUniversal" registry change on Windows. See https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/System_time#UTC_in_Microsoft_Windows

This is issue is mostly a request that users can choose to have that registry change pre-applied, if they believe their Windows/Windows ToGo install will have to co-exist with Linux installs, while the user is still interacting with the Rufus tool (IIRC it should fit on the Windows experience window)

Checklist

- [x] I looked at https://github.com/pbatard/rufus/wiki/FAQ to see if my question has already been answered.
- [x] I performed a search in the issue tracker for similar issues using keywords relevant to my problem, such as the error message I got from the log.
- [ ] I clicked the 'Log' button (🗒️) or pressed <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>-<kbd>L</kbd> in Rufus, or used [DebugView](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sysinternals/downloads/debugview), and copy/pasted the log into the section that says `<FULL LOG>` below.
- [ ] The log I am copying is the FULL log, starting with the line `Rufus version: x.y.z` - I have NOT removed any part of it.

Additionally (if applicable):
- [x] I ran a bad blocks check, by clicking _Show advanced format options_ then _Check device for bad blocks_, and confirmed that my USB is not defective.
- [x] I also tried one or more of the following:
  - [ ] Using a different USB drive.
  - [x] Plugging the USB into a different port.
  - [ ] Running Rufus on a different computer.
- [x] If using an image, I clicked on the `(✓)` button to compute the MD5, SHA1 and SHA256 checksums, which are therefore present in the log I copied. I confirmed, by performing an internet search, that these values match the ones from the official image.

Issue description
-----------------

If you enable that checkbox while creating a Ubuntu install media, your EFI/boot will look like this:
```
bootx64.efi
bootx64_original.efi
```
because it [inserts](https://github.com/pbatard/rufus/blob/eedeaea75ff103b2df0f306ed972244abaf46b58/src/hash.c#L2540) its own EFI file that first validates the media, and then executes the original EFI.

Then while installing Ubuntu, you'll get a very unhelpful "Something went wrong" screen as the installer tries to show you disk partitions, and then the install won't proceed any further.
If you dig hard enough through logs then you'll see it's caused by:

[unexpected multiple matches for installer shim obtained using globbing pattern ".*/cdrom/EFI/boot/boot\*.efi"](https://github.com/canonical/snapd/blob/0d33809c79cd41749d09949f5222e87171e17693/overlord/install/install_test.go#L311)
which was introduced in snapd by [this commit](https://github.com/canonical/snapd/commit/5552ba92856822369539b6c58ffe4160f1b29be3) ([pull request](https://github.com/canonical/snapd/pull/15596)) in June 2025.

This is not an issue if you install Ubuntu without that checkbox checked, as then there's only one `bootx64.efi`.
I verified that by manually deleting `bootx64.efi` and then manually renaming `bootx64_original.efi` into `bootx64.efi`, after which the install succeeded without any other changes.

title: Getting an error from Microsoft Sentinel after retrying a download (download script code 4)
author: ozedri
body: Issue description ----------------- A Windows ISO download failed due to not having enough space, so I wanted to retry and save in another location with free space and got the following error: `Error: Sentinel marked this request as rejected.` According to the log the download script exited with code 4. Here's a screenshot of the error. The error is pretty straightforward - I got blocked since I tried to download again from Microsoft. I guess it also has something to do with the way the ISO is being downloaded. Just thought maybe we should get a simple, user-friendly error instead of the current one above as some users aren't familiar with Microsoft Sentinel so they won't be able to easily tell from the error why they got rejected. title: Feature Request/Improvement: Safety check for target device selection in "Windows User Experience" dialog
author: Aphernix258
body:
While using the latest version of Rufus, I encountered an issue where the target device selection was automatically set to my primary internal NVMe SSD instead of the USB flash drive I intended to use.

Although I understand the goal is to speed up the process, the "Windows User Experience" dialog box automatically applies settings that can lead to catastrophic data loss if the user doesn't manually double-check the "Device" dropdown menu.

The Problem:
In my case, the application defaulted to my internal 512GB SSD. Since I was rushing through the UI, I accidentally overwrote my drive. While I managed to recover some data, this is a significant UX issue.

Suggested Improvement:

    Clear Warning: Add a mandatory confirmation checkbox or a prominent warning dialog when an internal drive is selected as the target device.

    Default Selection: Change the logic so that Rufus defaults to "No selection" or forces the user to explicitly select an external USB device before the "Start" button becomes enabled.

    UI Highlight: If an internal drive is detected, highlight the "Device" field in yellow or red to warn the user that they are about to format a non-removable drive.

Please consider adding these safety guardrails to prevent other users from experiencing accidental data loss. Thanks for your great work on this tool!

title: [INTERNAL] List of rufus-4.16 enhancements and fixes
author: pbatard
body:
﻿﻿﻿﻿**This topic is flagged as INTERNAL and is therefore NOT open for comments.**

### Medium Term

- [ ] What the fudge is Microsoft doing with UEFI:NTFS in Hyper-V?
- [ ] In UEFI:NTFS (with Secure Boot enabled), do we want to take a peek at the SVN of the bootmgr bootloader and the system's SVN to warn users if the media they are trying to boot is going to fail security validation?
- [ ] Ah are you frigging kidding me! Microsoft updated their DBX a mere hours after the release of 4.15...
- [ ] We should attempt to detect when a DBX update has been saved but the files have now been deleted by the user.

### The ever-bounced lot

- [ ] on 24H2, USB Device Tree Viewer v4.4+ can show the actual USB 3 5/10/20G speed, and we sure want to be able to do the same in Rufus. How do they do it, knowing that Microsoft have not provided `IOCTL_USB_GET_NODE_CONNECTION_INFORMATION_EX_V3`?
- [ ] We get way too many device list refreshes after creating a blank UEFI:NTFS drive.
- [ ] We're going to have to take matters in our own hands when it comes to the [**buggy** AMI NTFS driver](https://github.com/pbatard/AmiNtfsBug) and forcefully prevent the non UEFI:NTFS partition to boot when md5sum validation is applied...
- [ ] Windows installer **corrupts** the backup GPT of the installer drive?!? What the hell?!? → _Seen when installing Win11 and shutting down the machine after the installer bails out on the 59fd550c468f13ab086076061f26f9ce15a93d51 issue_

### Long Term No Rush

- [ ] Do we want to push the GDIE (`grub_debug_is_enabled`) to mainline GRUB, since Fedora are NOT bothering with that? We could also push for an autolookup of `grub.cfg` under the prefixed dir (which we could set to root) instead of having to contend with `/boot/grub`, `/boot/grub2` and whatnot.
- [ ] Codename "Washington"
- [ ] There's an [f3probe on Linux](https://i.redd.it/iyaie65pd3qf1.jpeg) to detect fake drives. Could we pick up on it for Rufus?
- [ ] Ultimately, we probably want to replace all mentions of _ISO Mode_ with _FST Mode_...
- [ ] Do we have an issue [unloading old UEFI:NTFS drivers](https://i.redd.it/d5uvbc646q2d1.jpeg) (and have Google secretly been using our NTFS drivers in their Chromebooks)? → _Needs more data... which we don't have!_
- [ ] Add a `.esp` image copy mode?
- [ ] Is there any way we can make the regular MSVC exe behave the same as the App Store one? Even as it's the same executable binary, the App Store version is a lot less forgiving about exceptions and at any rate, you'd expect the standard MSVC binary to report stuff like access violations, especially when debugging in VS, yet it doesn't...
- [ ] The case of tails and its early write-error unless you zero the drive first... And now the case of Windows complaining that it needs a restart when you write a UEFI bootloader.
- [ ] Should we try to remove volume drive letter while saving to VHD just to be safe? We do lock the physical drive, but Windows being Windows, we may still have some bad surprises there...
- [ ] Async/double buffering improvements for ISO mode
- [ ] Use VDS for `GetDrivePartitionData()` and use this opportunity to call on `IVdsVolumeMF_GetFileSystemProperties()` to get the file systems. ~~Or we might switch to using WMI as Microsoft advises and create our own C++ wmi library interface.~~ (Except WMI won't for Win7 users...)
- [ ] Also we should use VDS to query data for label sanitization
- [ ] Add support for `.json` provided custom commands to apply after the image has been created. e.g. something like:
```json
{
  "commands": [
    {
      "image": "en_windows_7_ultimate_with_sp1_x86_dvd_619077.iso",
      "source_dir": "C:/my_dir/",
      "custom_var1": "SOME_VAR",
      "command": "my_command.exe some_flags %custom_var1% some_more_flags"
    }
  ]
}
```
- [ ] We don't detect Ubuntu ARM64 as Linux images where persistence can be enabled. We may want to change that.
- [ ] UI should be disabled during port cycle until we detect the cycled device again (with timeout in case the user yanked the device)
- [ ] Label changed by the user should not revert to ISO label once the drive has been created.

As an active contributor to the open-source community (and lead developer of Artado Search), I have recently been focusing on modernizing the web interfaces of critical utilities. To facilitate this, I have built a fully functional frontend preview prototype from scratch to demonstrate a potential modern direction for the Rufus web experience.

title: ‘&’ in local account username breaks the generated unattend.xml
author: christian8641
body: Checklist --------- - [x] I have been made aware that if my problem can be summarized as _"I've created or tried to create a media using Rufus, and ..."_, and I am not including a log, **this issue will be summarily closed**. - [x] I looked at https://github.com/pbatard/rufus/wiki/FAQ to see if my question has already been answered. - [x] I performed a search in the issue tracker for similar issues using keywords relevant to my problem, such as the error message I got from the log. - [x] I clicked the 'Log' button (🗒️) or pressed Ctrl-L in Rufus, or used [DebugView](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sysinternals/downloads/debugview), and copy/pasted the log into the section that says `` below. - [x] The log I am copying is the FULL log, starting with the line `Rufus version: x.y.z` - I have NOT removed any part of it. Issue description ----------------- Same kind of source bug as #2984, also in src/wue.c. No useful log: the Rufus run completes without error. The problem is in the generated unattend.xml (attached). A '&' in the local account username is written into the XML as-is, but XML requires `&`. The file is then not valid XML. Why it gets through: - src/stdlg.c line 849 replaces `/\[]:;|=,+*?<>"` - src/wue.c line 352 filters `/\[]:|<>+=;,?*%@.` Neither catches '&', and per the Microsoft doc linked at line 350, '&' is allowed in account names. The username is written unescaped into `` and `` (lines 362-363) and the net user command (line 380). Line 396 already escapes `&&` as `&&`, so this looks like the only spot missing it. Tested with Rufus , Windows 11 ISO, username "test & test": media creation succeeds, the install runs through the phase that needs the USB, then fails with an error. OK reboots, same error again - it never completes. Short username without &, same ISO, same VM: installs fine. Fix: escape '&' as `&` when writing the username (like line 396), or add '&' to the filter lists (simpler, but blocks a character Windows allows). The `` at line 340 is written unescaped too - probably never an issue with real timezone names, but same pattern. Log --- ``` No log - the run completes without error, the bug is in the attached unattend.xml ``` [unattend.xml](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/28902470/unattend.xml)
